hypothesis: Enhancing aromatic complementarity at position 5 with SSTR2-specific ECL2 residues (e.g., Phe193) and introducing hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will improve SSTR2 selectivity by strengthening subtype-specific contacts while avoiding off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating strong SSTR2 binders but not sufficiently disrupting off-target interactions. The next iteration will focus on optimizing non-pharmacophore positions to enhance selectivity while maintaining binding affinity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, we must disrupt conserved contacts at non-pharmacophore positions (1, 2, 5, 6, 11, 12) that are likely responsible for off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.
The current ddG threshold is too lenient, allowing candidates with weak binding to pass. Tightening the ddG threshold will ensure only the strongest binders are selected.
